meta.revision_note: Part 1: na.sa (H5375O) and da.var (H1697H) have no verse records by researcher decision. Phase 2 note recorded in data_quality_flags for both terms.

term_inventory_greek.status_note: NO_WORD_ANALYSIS: STEP returns no analysis for epithumētos. Strong's provisional.
NO_WORD_ANALYSIS: STEP returns no analysis for potheinos.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: desire, longing (in contexts where the desire is positive and proper); coveting, craving, lusting (in contexts where the desire is immoral and sinful); earnest desire, Lk. 22:15; irregular or violent desire, Mk. 4:19; specifically impure desire, lust, Rom. 1:24; metaphorically the object of desire, what enkindles desire, 1Jn. 2:16, 17
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: epithumia: desire, yearning; appetite; sexual desire, lust; with genitive, longing after a thing; object of desire.
term_inventory_greek.status_note: LSJ: ἐπιθυμία — desire, yearning; appetite; especially sexual desire, lust; with genitive, longing after a thing.
NO_WORD_ANALYSIS: STEP returns no analysis for epithumēma. Strong's provisional.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: goodwill, favor, good purpose; good will, favor, Lk. 2:14; good pleasure, purpose, intention, Mt. 11:26; Lk. 10:21; Eph. 1:5, 9; Phil. 2:13; by implication desire, Rom. 10:1; Phil. 1:15; 2Thess. 1:11
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: eudokia: especially of God; good will; contentment; object of desire.
term_inventory_greek.status_note: LSJ: goodwill; contentment; object of desire. god_as_subject=true: Lk 2:14, Mt 11:26, Eph 1:5, Phil 2:13.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: longing, desire; earnest desire, Rom. 15:23
term_inventory_greek.status_note: LSJ: = epipothēsis.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: longing (for); earnest desire, strong affection, 2Cor. 7:7, 11
term_inventory_greek.status_note: LSJ: longing after.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: lust, desire; desire, longing; lust, concupiscence, Rom. 1:27
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: orexis: general word for all kinds of appetency, conation, including epithumia, thumos, boulesis; longing or yearning after a thing; propension, appetency.
term_inventory_greek.status_note: STRONGS_RECONCILED: MTI provisional G3713 corrected to G3715 (orexis). oregō (to aspire) is G3713 — separate lemma. LSJ: general word for all kinds of appetency, conation, including epithumia, thumos, boulēsis.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: one who desires, sets heart on (evil); one who has an ardent desire for, anything, 1Cor. 10:6
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: epithumetes: one who longs for or desires; lover, follower; one who lusts.
term_inventory_greek.status_note: STRONGS_RECONCILED: MTI provisional PENDING-043-epithumetes corrected to G1938. LSJ: one who longs for or desires; lover, follower; one who lusts.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: plan, will, choice; purpose, will, determination, choice Acts 27:43; Rom. 9:19; 1Pet. 4:3
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: boulema: purpose; intent; meaning; will, testament; express will, consent.
term_inventory_greek.status_note: god_as_subject=true: Rom 9:19 — resisting God's will. LSJ: purpose; intent; meaning; will, testament.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: pleasing, desirable, right; pleasing, acceptable, Acts 12:3; 1Jn. 3:22; 8:29; deemed proper, desirable, right Acts 6:2
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: arestos: acceptable, pleasing; satisfactory; of persons, acceptable, approved.
term_inventory_greek.status_note: god_as_subject=true: Joh 8:29 — doing what pleases the Father.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: to ask, request; demand; desire, Acts 7:46
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: aiteō: ask, beg; ask for, demand; ask a person for a thing; postulate, assume.
term_inventory_greek.status_note: SMALL_VERSE_SAMPLE: 70 occurrences; selected verses cover the prayer/petition and demand senses.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: to ask; beg, urge; pray; to ask, interrogate, inquire of, Mt. 21:24; Lk. 20:3; in NT to ask, request, beg, beseech, Mt. 15:23; Lk. 4:38; Jn. 14:16
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: erōtaō: ask; ask about a thing; question a person; challenge; beg, entreat.
term_inventory_greek.status_note: god_as_subject=true: Joh 14:16 — Jesus asking the Father. SMALL_VERSE_SAMPLE: 59 occurrences; selected verses cover key senses.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: (middle) to set one's heart on, strive for, aspire to, desire; to extend, stretch out; middle to stretch one's self out, to reach forward to, metaphorically to desire earnestly, long after, 1Tim. 3:1; Heb. 11:16; by implication to indulge in, be devoted to, 1Tim. 6:10
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: oregō: reach, stretch out; middle: stretch oneself out, reach at or to a thing, grasp at, yearn for; metaphorically reach after, yearn for.
term_inventory_greek.status_note: STRONGS_RECONCILED: MTI provisional PENDING-043-oregō confirmed as G3713. orexis (G3715) and oregō (G3713) are cognates — noun and verb of same root.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: (middle) to ask for; to demand; middle to demand for one's self, Lk. 22:31; also, to obtain by asking
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: exaiteō: demand or ask for from another; demand the surrender of a person; middle: ask for oneself, demand.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: to be filled with desires that conflict with dedication to someone; to be headstrong, or wanton towards, feel sexual desire 1Tim. 5:11
to desire, long for; to desire, wish for, long for
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: himeirō: long for, desire; Epic/Ionic/Tragic word.
term_inventory_greek.status_note: LSJ: long for, desire; Epic/Ionic/Tragic word.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: to long for, crave, desire; to desire besides; also, to desire earnestly, long for, 2Cor. 5:2; to have a strong bent, Jas. 4:5; by implication to love, have affection for, 2Cor. 9:14
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: epipotheō: desire besides or yearn after; feel the want of.
term_inventory_greek.status_note: god_as_subject=true: Jam 4:5 — God yearns jealously over the spirit he has made to dwell in us.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: to long for, desire; covet, lust; with the genitive or accusative, to set the heart upon; to desire, long for, have earnest desire, Mt. 13:17; Lk. 15:16; to lust after, Mt. 5:28; specifically to covet, Rom. 13:9
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: epithumeō: set one's heart upon a thing, long for, covet, desire; desire to do; absolutely, desire, covet.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: to wish, will, desire; to choose, determine, plan; to be willing, disposed, Mk. 15:15; Acts 25:20; 28:18; to intend, Mt. 1:19; Acts 5:28; 12:4; 2Cor. 1:15; to desire, 1Tim. 6:9; to choose, be pleased, Jn. 18:39; Acts 18:15; Jas. 3:4; to will, decree, appoint, Lk. 22:42; Jas. 1:18; 1Cor. 12:11; 1Tim. 2:8; 5:14
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: boulomai: will, wish, be willing; prefer; mean, profess; tend to be; to be wont.
term_inventory_greek.status_note: god_as_subject=true: Lk 22:42, Jam 1:18, Heb 6:17.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: to ask, beg, plead; to comfort, encourage, exhort, urge; to call, invite; to call for, invite to come, send for, Acts 28:20; to call upon, exhort, admonish, persuade, Lk. 3:18; Acts 2:40; 11:23; to beg, beseech, entreat, implore, Mt. 8:5, 31; 18:29; Mk. 1:40; to animate, encourage, comfort, console, Mt. 2:18; 5:4; 2Cor. 1:4, 6; passive to be cheered, comforted, Lk. 16:25; Acts 20:12; 2Cor. 7:13
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: parakaleō: call to one; call in, summon; exhort, encourage; comfort, console; excite; demand, require; beseech, entreat; passive: relent; repent.
term_inventory_greek.status_note: SMALL_VERSE_SAMPLE: 109 occurrences; selected verses cover pleading, comfort, and exhortation senses. Cross-registry note: comfort/consolation senses overlap with grief (071) and sorrow (154) registries.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: to seek, search for; to seek out, look for; to desire, demand, require; to enquire after, investigate
term_inventory_greek.status_note: THIN_DATA: occurrence count not stated in source. god_as_subject=true: implied in God seeking godly offspring (Mal 2:15 context).

term_inventory_greek.meaning: to seek after, seek for; to desire, covet, demand
term_inventory_greek.status_note: THIN_DATA: occurrence count not stated in source.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: to will, wish, desire; to be willing, disposed; to intend; to choose; to decree, appoint
term_inventory_greek.status_note: SMALL_VERSE_SAMPLE: occurrence count not stated in source; large NT term. god_as_subject=true: extensive — God's will is a primary theme throughout. LSJ: prefer (boulesthai vs thelein distinction noted in source).

term_inventory_greek.meaning: will, desire; purpose, counsel; what one wishes or has determined shall be done
term_inventory_greek.status_note: god_as_subject=true: dominant NT usage — will of God/Father/Christ throughout. SMALL_VERSE_SAMPLE: occurrence count not stated in source; large NT term.

verse_records.verse_text: but the cares of the world and the deceitfulness of riches and the desires for other things enter in and choke the word, and it proves unfruitful.
And he said to them, 'I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.'
You are of your father the devil, and your will is to do your father's desires. He was a murderer from the beginning.
Therefore God gave them up in the lusts of their hearts to impurity, to the dishonoring of their bodies among themselves,
Let not sin therefore reign in your mortal body, to make you obey its passions.

What then shall we say? That the law is sin? By no means! Yet if it had not been for the law, I would not have known sin. For I would not have known what it is to covet if the law had not said, 'You shall not covet.'
But sin, seizing an opportunity through the commandment, produced in me all kinds of covetousness.
But put on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no provision for the flesh, to gratify its desires.
But I say, walk by the Spirit, and you will not gratify the desires of the flesh.
And those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the flesh with its passions and desires.
among whom we all once lived in the passions of our flesh, carrying out the desires of the flesh and the mind.
to put off your old self, which belongs to your former manner of life and is corrupt through deceitful desires,
I am hard pressed between the two. My desire is to depart and be with Christ, for that is far better.
Put to death therefore what is earthly in you: sexual immorality, impurity, passion, evil desire, and covetousness, which is idolatry.
But since we were torn away from you, brothers, for a short time, in person not in heart, we endeavored the more eagerly and with great desire to see you face to face,
not in the passion of lust like the Gentiles who do not know God;
But those who desire to be rich fall into temptation, into a snare, into many senseless and harmful desires that plunge people into ruin and destruction.
So flee youthful passions and pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace.
For among them are those who creep into households and capture weak women, burdened with sins and led astray by various passions,
For the time is coming when people will not endure sound teaching, but having itching ears they will accumulate for themselves teachers to suit their own passions,
training us to renounce ungodliness and worldly passions, and to live self-controlled, upright, and godly lives in the present age,
For we ourselves were once foolish, disobedient, led astray, slaves to various passions and pleasures.
But each person is tempted when he is lured and enticed by his own desire.
Then desire when it has conceived gives birth to sin, and sin when it is fully grown brings forth death.
As obedient children, do not be conformed to the passions of your former ignorance,
Beloved, I urge you as sojourners and exiles to abstain from the passions of the flesh, which wage war against your soul.
so as to live for the rest of the time in the flesh no longer for human passions but for the will of God.
For the time that is past suffices for doing what the Gentiles want to do, living in sensuality, passions, drunkenness, orgies, drinking parties, and lawless idolatry.

by which he has granted to us his precious and very great promises, so that through them you may become partakers of the divine nature, having escaped from the corruption that is in the world because of sinful desire.
and especially those who indulge in the lust of defiling passion and despise authority.
For, speaking loud boasts of folly, they entice by sensual passions of the flesh those who are barely escaping from those who live in error.
knowing this first of all, that scoffers will come in the last days with scoffing, following their own sinful desires.
For all that is in the world—the desires of the flesh and the desires of the eyes and pride of life—is not from the Father but is from the world.
And the world is passing away along with its desires, but whoever does the will of God abides forever.
These are grumblers, malcontents, following their own sinful desires; they are loud-mouthed boasters, showing favoritism to gain advantage.
They said to you, 'In the last time there will be scoffers, following their own ungodly passions.'
The fruit for which your soul longed has gone from you, and all your delicacies and your splendors are lost to you, never to be found again!
yes, Father, for such was your gracious will.
Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace among those with whom he is pleased!

In that same hour he rejoiced in the Holy Spirit and said, 'I thank you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that you have hidden these things from the wise and understanding and revealed them to little children; yes, Father, for such was your gracious will.'
Brothers, my heart's desire and prayer to God for them is that they may be saved.
he predestined us for adoption to himself as sons through Jesus Christ, according to the purpose of his will,
making known to us the mystery of his will, according to his purpose, which he set forth in Christ
Some indeed preach Christ from envy and rivalry, but others from good will.
for it is God who works in you, both to will and to work for his good pleasure.
To this end we always pray for you, that our God may make you worthy of his calling and may fulfill every resolve for good and every work of faith by his power,
But now, since I no longer have any room for work in these regions, and since I have longed for many years to come to you,
and not only by his coming but also by the comfort with which he was comforted by you, as he told us of your longing, your mourning, your zeal for me, so that I rejoiced still more.
For see what earnestness this godly grief has produced in you, but also what eagerness to clear yourselves, what indignation, what fear, what longing, what zeal, what punishment!

and the men likewise gave up natural relations with women and were consumed with passion for one another, men committing shameless acts with men and receiving in themselves the due penalty for their error.
Now these things took place as examples for us, that we might not desire evil as they did.
But the centurion, wishing to save Paul, kept them from carrying out their plan. He ordered those who could swim to jump overboard first and make for the land,
You will say to me then, 'Why does he still find fault? For who can resist his will?'
And he who sent me is with me. He has not left me alone, for I always do the things that are pleasing to him.
And the twelve summoned the full number of the disciples and said, 'It is not right that we should give up preaching the word of God to serve tables.'
and when he saw that it pleased the Jews, he proceeded to arrest Peter also. This was during the days of Unleavened Bread.
and whatever we ask we receive from him, because we keep his commandments and do what pleases him.
Give to the one who begs from you, and do not refuse the one who would borrow from you.
Do not be like them, for your Father knows what you need before you ask him.
Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and it will be opened to you.
If you then, who are evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father who is in heaven give good things to those who ask him!
so that he promised with an oath to give her whatever she might ask.
Again I say to you, if two of you agree on earth about anything they ask, it will be done for them by my Father in heaven.
And whatever you ask in prayer, you will receive, if you have faith.
He went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus. Then Pilate ordered it to be given to him.
For when Herodias's daughter came in and danced, she pleased Herod and his guests. And the king said to the girl, 'Ask me for whatever you wish, and I will give it to you.'
Therefore I tell you, whatever you ask in prayer, believe that you have received it, and it will be yours.
And I tell you, ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and it will be opened to you.
This man went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.
Jesus answered her, 'If you knew the gift of God, and who it is that is saying to you, Give me a drink, you would have asked him, and he would have given you living water.'
Whatever you ask in my name, this I will do, that the Father may be glorified in the Son.
If you abide in me, and my words abide in you, ask whatever you wish, and it will be done for you.
In that day you will ask nothing of me. Truly, truly, I say to you, whatever you ask of the Father in my name, he will give it to you.
who found favor in the sight of God and asked to find a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.
Then they asked for a king, and God gave them Saul the son of Kish, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, for forty years.
If any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask God, who gives generously to all without reproach, and it will be given him.
You desire and do not have, so you murder. You covet and cannot obtain, so you fight and quarrel. You do not have, because you do not ask.
You ask and do not receive, because you ask wrongly, to spend it on your passions.
And this is the confidence that we have toward him, that if we ask anything according to his will he hears us.
But he did not answer her a word. And his disciples came and begged him, saying, 'Send her away, for she is crying out after us.'
Now when Jesus came into the district of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, 'Who do people say that the Son of Man is?'
Jesus answered them, 'I also will ask you one question, and if you tell me the answer, then I also will tell you by what authority I do these things.'
And he arose and left the synagogue and entered Simon's house. Now Simon's mother-in-law was ill with a high fever, and they appealed to him on her behalf.
When the centurion heard about Jesus, he sent to him elders of the Jews, asking him to come and heal his servant.
One of the Pharisees asked him to eat with him, and he went into the Pharisee's house and reclined at table.
But they all alike began to make excuses. The first said to him, 'I have bought a field, and I must go out and see it. Please have me excused.'
And this is the testimony of John, when the Jews sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, 'Who are you?'
And I will ask the Father, and he will give you another Helper, to be with you forever,
In that day you will ask nothing of me. Truly, truly, I say to you, whatever you ask of the Father in my name, he will give it to you.
In that day you will ask in my name, and I do not say to you that I will ask the Father on your behalf;
I am praying for them. I am not praying for the world but for those whom you have given me, for they are yours.
I do not ask for these only, but also for those who will believe in me through their word,
Yes, I ask you also, true companion, help these women, who have labored side by side with me in the gospel.
Finally, then, brothers, we ask and urge you in the Lord Jesus, that as you received from us how you ought to walk and to please God.
If anyone sees his brother committing a sin not leading to death, he shall ask, and God will give him life.
And now I ask you, dear lady—not as though I were writing you a new commandment, but the one we have had from the beginning—that we love one another.
The saying is trustworthy: If anyone aspires to the office of overseer, he desires a noble task.
For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evils. It is through this craving that some have wandered away from the faith and pierced themselves with many pangs.
But as it is, they desire a better country, that is, a heavenly one. Therefore God is not ashamed to be called their God, for he has prepared for them a city.
Simon, Simon, behold, Satan demanded to have you, that he might sift you like wheat,
But refuse to enroll younger widows, for when their passions draw them away from Christ, they desire to marry
So, being affectionately desirous of you, we were ready to share with you not only the gospel of God but also our own selves, because you had become very dear to us.
For I long to see you, that I may impart to you some spiritual gift to strengthen you—
For in this tent we groan, longing to put on our heavenly dwelling,
while they long for you and pray for you, because of the surpassing grace of God upon you.
For God is my witness, how I yearn for you all with the affection of Christ Jesus.
for he has been longing for you all and has been distressed because you heard that he was ill.
But now that Timothy has come to us from you, and has brought us the good news of your faith and love and reported that you always remember us kindly and long to see us, as we long to see you—
As I remember your tears, I long to see you, that I may be filled with joy.
Or do you suppose it is to no purpose that the Scripture says, 'He yearns jealously over the spirit that he has made to dwell in us'?
Like newborn infants, long for the pure spiritual milk, that by it you may grow up into salvation—
But I say to you that everyone who looks at a woman with lustful intent has already committed adultery with her in his heart.
For truly, I say to you, many prophets and righteous people longed to see what you see, and did not see it, and to hear what you hear, and did not hear it.
And he was longing to be fed with the pods that the pigs ate, and no one gave him anything.
who desired to be fed with what fell from the rich man's table.
And he said to the disciples, 'The days are coming when you will desire to see one of the days of the Son of Man, and you will not see it.'
And he said to them, 'I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.'
I coveted no one's silver or gold or apparel.

What then shall we say? That the law is sin? By no means! Yet if it had not been for the law, I would not have known sin. For I would not have known what it is to covet if the law had not said, 'You shall not covet.'

Now these things took place as examples for us, that we might not desire evil as they did.
For the desires of the flesh are against the Spirit, and the desires of the Spirit are against the flesh, for these are opposed to each other.

saying, 'Father, if you are willing, remove this cup from me. Nevertheless, not my will, but yours, be done.'

and implored him earnestly, saying, 'My little daughter is at the point of death. Come and lay your hands on her, so that she may be made well and live.'

who comforts us in all our affliction, so that we may be able to comfort those who are in any affliction, with the comfort with which we ourselves are comforted by God.

no one understands; no one seeks for God.

cross_registry_links.note: Part 2 Greek terms should be read in conjunction with Part 1 Hebrew terms. Root family labels from Part 1 are carried in root_families_in_prior_parts.
parakaleō (G3870) comfort/consolation senses overlap with grief (071) and sorrow (154).
anxiety (007) merimna cluster and desire (043) epithumia cluster are semantically adjacent in NT passages about divided heart (Lk 21:34; Phil 4:6).
